title: Azure Data Factory Mapping API Endpoint
story: As a data migration specialist, I want to call a DataTools Pro API endpoint that returns a structured JSON mapping compatible with Azure Data Factory, so that I can automate data pipeline configuration between Salesforce and Snowflake without manually building mapping files.
description:
Implement a new REST API endpoint that generates Azure Data Factory-compatible mapping JSON from DataTools Pro's existing data dictionary metadata.

### Background

Currently a Snowflake stored procedure (`SFDC.STAGE.ADFMAPPINGFROMSFDCMETADATA`) generates TabularTranslator mapping JSON by querying Salesforce metadata stored in Snowflake. This maps source fields to sink fields with appropriate data type conversions (e.g., Salesforce `datetime` → ADF `Date/Time`, `boolean` → `Boolean`).

### API Design

**Endpoint:** `POST /api/v1.0/azure_mapping`

**Headers:**
- `Authorization: Bearer <token>` (generated from the app)

**Request Body:**
```json
{
  "salesforce_instance": "https://example.my.salesforce.com",
  "salesforce_object": "Lead",
  "Snowflake_instance": "rsa34655.east-us-2.azure",
  "Snowflake_object": "Lead"
}
```

**Response:** TabularTranslator-compatible JSON with source/sink field mappings and data type conversions.

### Data Type Mapping

| Salesforce Type | ADF Type |
|----------------|----------|
| id | String |
| string | String |
| boolean | Boolean |
| double | Decimal |
| integer | Integer |
| datetime | Date/Time |
| date | Date/Time |
| address | String |

The endpoint leverages the data mappings already configured within DataTools Pro between Salesforce objects and Snowflake objects. A unique identifier must be passed to select the correct mapping when multiple mappings exist.
acceptance_criteria: API endpoint POST /api/v1.0/azure_mapping accepts Salesforce and Snowflake instance/object parameters
Response returns valid TabularTranslator JSON with source/sink field mappings
Data types are correctly converted between Salesforce and Snowflake type systems
Authentication via Bearer token is required
Unmapped metadata fields are excluded from the response
Multiple mappings for the same object pair can be distinguished by a unique identifier
Endpoint works with mappings configured in the DataTools Pro UI
ux_changes: Users configure Salesforce-to-Snowflake field mappings through the DataTools Pro UI by selecting integration, object, and mapping source/sink fields before the API can return the mapping.
middleware_changes: New controller action handles the azure_mapping endpoint, queries the configured field mappings for the specified Salesforce and Snowflake objects, applies data type conversions, and serializes the result as TabularTranslator JSON.
api_changes: POST /api/v1.0/azure_mapping — accepts {salesforce_instance, salesforce_object, Snowflake_instance, Snowflake_object}. Returns ADF TabularTranslator JSON with source/sink mappings and data types. Requires Bearer token authentication.
implementation_notes: Replaces existing Snowflake stored procedure SFDC.STAGE.ADFMAPPINGFROMSFDCMETADATA with a DataTools Pro API endpoint. The mapping leverages metadata already configured in the app's data dictionary. Implementation confirmed working on dev environment via Postman testing.
comment_summary: Waqar confirmed the JSON structure direction and asked about handling unmapped metadata fields. Implementation was completed with a working POST endpoint at /api/v1.0/azure_mapping, tested via Postman on the dev environment with Salesforce Lead to Snowflake Lead mapping.
